framework.name: SPIFFE solves identity, not delegation
framework.description: Posta explicitly scopes SPIFFE to the identity/authentication layer ('who is this agent?') but stops before authorization/delegation ('what can it do on behalf of whom?'). The article ends at 'how can you possibly write authorization policies around [fine-grained identities]?' — acknowledging SPIFFE handles hop-1 identity but punts on the hop-2+ delegation problem entirely.
framework.raw_quote: if identities are more fine-grained, and even potentially generated on the fly, how can you possibly write authorization policies around this?

source.title: Agent Identity and Access Management - Can SPIFFE Work?
